message.reasoning_content:
We need to solve the problem: Given n ≥ 3 integer, determine all nonnegative integers k such that there exist n distinct lines in the plane satisfying:

(1) For all positive integers a,b with a+b ≤ n+1, the point (a,b) lies on at least one of the lines.

(2) Exactly k of the n lines are sunny. A line is sunny if it is not parallel to the x-axis, y-axis, or the line x+y=0. So a line is sunny if its slope is not 0, undefined (vertical), or -1. So sunny lines have slopes not
